title: Ensure that the controller manager pod specification file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the controller manager pod specification file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the controller manager pod specification file ownership is set to root:root.
input_row.rationale: The controller manager pod specification file controls various parameters that set the behavior of various components of the master node. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml Verify that the ownership is set to root:root.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml Default Value: By default, kube-controller-manager.yaml file ownership is set to root:root. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-controller-manager
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Master Node •  Level 2 - Master Node

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control requires verification of the file ownership (root:root) of the kube-controller-manager pod specification file on the host filesystem (e.g., /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml). Kubernetes APIs and kubectl do not expose file system ownership or permissions of manifest files on the host. This property can only be checked by accessing the underlying node OS, as the audit procedure requires running stat or similar commands directly on the node. Therefore, this check cannot be automated via Kubernetes APIs or kubectl.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the Kubernetes control plane node where the kube-controller-manager runs.
2. Locate the kube-controller-manager pod specification file (commonly at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml).
3. Run the following command to check the file ownership:
   ```
   stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml
   ```
4. Verify that the output is:
   ```
   root:root
   ```
5. If the ownership is not root:root, remediate by running:
   ```
   chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml
   ```
6. Confirm compliance by re-running the stat command and ensuring the output is root:root.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
